description: Fix a bug in the CLI logs follow mode where concurrent file writes cause duplicate log output.

userStories.title: Fix followFile duplicate log output on concurrent writes
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want opentabs logs -f to not print duplicate log lines when the server writes to the log file while the follow reader is active.
acceptanceCriteria: followFile tracks actual bytes consumed (sum of chunk lengths) rather than using the stale statSync currentSize as the new offset
offset is updated to start + actual bytes read on stream end, not to the pre-read currentSize
New test verifies that log content written during an active read cycle is not duplicated on the next cycle
userStories.notes: In platform/cli/src/commands/logs.ts:92-146, the followFile function captures currentSize via statSync at line 99 before opening the read stream at line 112. The stream reads to actual EOF (which may be beyond currentSize if the server wrote more data during the read). On 'end' event at line 130-131, offset is set to currentSize (the stale pre-read value), not to the actual bytes consumed. This causes bytes between currentSize and actual EOF to be re-read and re-printed on the next cycle. Fix: track actual bytes consumed by summing chunk.length in the 'data' handler (using Buffer.byteLength for string chunks), then set offset = startOffset + actualBytesRead on 'end'. The startOffset is the value of offset at the time createReadStream was called (line 112). Alternatively, track bytesRead by replacing line 131 with offset += stream.bytesRead (but check if createReadStream exposes bytesRead). Tests would go in platform/cli/src/commands/logs.test.ts.
